title: plumbaginaceae
intro: The Plumbaginaceae rank is a family of flowering plants. It is a group of approximately 90 genera and 900 species, commonly known as sea lavenders, leadworts, or plumbagos. These plants are typically found in coastal and mountainous regions, and are known for their delicate, bell-shaped flowers and often silvery or gray foliage. The family Plumbaginaceae is a diverse and widespread group, with species found on every continent except Antarctica.
